rationale: The seven-to-ten-day change is applied coherently to both the closure-SLA guard and penalty arithmetic. Ten is a nearby legacy grid value, so the resulting stale window is credible maintenance drift rather than a partial mechanical edit.

The edit only widens unused report-padding storage in BOIDENT3; compiled behavior and the partnership threshold classifier are unchanged. Reserved FILLER and print buffers are ordinary legacy COBOL layout maintenance.

The changed FILLER is behavior-inert reserved storage and the regulated KYC scheduling logic is untouched. Adjacent padding declarations are mundane in fixed-format report layouts despite the judge's missing-history uncertainty.
Changing the width of an unused print-line buffer is a natural report-layout maintenance edit. It compiles and does not affect the KYC due-year computation or any regulated branch.
The report-heading width change is an inert layout adjustment in CICREP1. It neither changes the thirty-day settlement rule nor the dispute gate and has no conspicuous mutation fingerprint.
